LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BILL WONG, CHOR MAN WONG, WING TAT WONG AND CHUNG WAH WONG'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED AS TO THE FOLLOWING PHRASE IN PARAGRAPH 2 OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT: "AS WELL AS IN THEIR OWN INDIVIDUAL CAPACITIES AS MEMBERS AND OFFICERS OF THE ASSOCIATION." PLAINTIFFS CHI WONG AND JACKSON WONG LACK STANDING TO SUE, AS NO HARM HAS BEEN ALLEGED TO EITHER PLAINTIFF AS AN INDIVIDUAL. (CCP 367.) PLAINTIFF HAS ALREADY BEEN GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING HARM TO INDIVIDUALS BY ORDER FILED DECEMBER 3, 2013, AND DID NOT DO SO. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTS BILL WONG, CHOR MAN WONG, WING TAT WONG and CHUNG WAH WONG'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted as to the following phrase in paragraph 2 of the Second Amended Complaint: "as well as in their own individual capacities as members and officers of the association." Plaintiffs Chi Wong and Jackson Wong lack standing to sue, as no harm has been alleged to either plaintiff as an individual. (CCP 367.) Plaintiff has already been given leave to amend to allege facts showing harm to individuals by Order filed December 3, 2013, and did not do so. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

DEFENDANTS BILL WONG, CHOR MAN WONG, WING TAT WONG and CHUNG WAH WONG'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied as moot in light of the ruling on the motion to strike. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-15-2014 Continued To Apr-22-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS BILL WONG, CHOR MAN WONG, WING TAT WONG and CHUNG WAH WONG's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to April 22, 2014 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than April 15, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-28-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS BILL WONG, CHOR MAN WONG, WING TAT WONG AND CHUNG WAH WONG'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. No opposition timely filed. The opposition was not filed until January 22, 2014, in violation of CCP 1005(b), which provides that opposition papers must be filed and served nine court days in advance of the hearing date. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-03-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS BILL WONG, CHOR MAN WONG, WING TAT WONG, and CHUNG WAH WONG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The Complaint fails to state a cause of action. (CCP 430.10(e).) The Complaint is uncertain. (CCP 430.10(f)).) Inconsistent facts are pled as to the status of the Defendant directors. (Complaint, 13, 18.) Plaintiff Chi Wong lacks standing to sue, as no harm has been alleged to him as an individual. (CCP 367.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction (Opposition Due 10/30; Reply Due 11/04)
Off Calendar Nov-06-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. GERARD LAM, TEL. NO. (510) 465-6685 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. JOHN K. KAO, TEL. NO. 362-9390 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction - The Court denies plaintiff's application for a preliminary injunction. Plaintiff does not plead a cognizable cause of action. Plaintiff fails to demonstrate a likelihood of prevailing on the merits and the Court finds defendant's version of the by-laws to be more persuasive. Plaintiff fails to demonstrate irreparable harm or explain why they did not seek relief sooner given that the alleged removal occurred in August of 2013. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)
